pattern: Each symbol is the inverse of its parent in the previous row, so the answer is the parity of the one-bits in the zero-based index.
hints: The kth symbol in row n comes from a single symbol in row n-1; track which parent produced it.
Position k maps to parent (k+1)//2, and a symbol equals its parent when k is odd, the flip when k is even.
Convert k to a zero-based index k-1; the answer is just the number of set bits in that index taken modulo two.

edge_inputs.description: first row first symbol is the seed zero
second row first symbol stays zero
second row second symbol flips to one
third row spells the four symbols 0110
index with two set bits returns zero
deeper row, odd position follows its parent
large row with a mid-range index

stress.description: random valid (n, k) pairs keeping k within 2^(n-1)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 30)
    k = rng.randint(1, 1 << (n - 1))
    return [n, k]
stress.note: k is bounded by the row length 2^(n-1)
